summaries.152040: Comments confirm the bug also reproduces with `requestFocusOnTap: true`, not just `false`. Reproduced on both stable 3.22.2 and master 3.24.0-pre on macOS. No fix or workaround proposed; issue remains open and stalled.
summaries.81201: Comments confirm reproduction on stable and master on web (Chrome/Edge on Windows). One commenter could not reproduce with NordPass or FirefoxRelay extensions. The misalignment is caused by password-manager browser extensions injecting icons without respecting Flutter's canvas coordinate system. No proposed fix; stalled.
summaries.174159: Comments confirm the wrong composing range (not resetting to -1,-1 after IME commit) affects web and iOS, while Android and macOS work correctly. A likely regression from #161593 is identified, rooted in a behavioral difference between Chinese and Japanese IMEs. A workaround using `TextEditingController.addListener` instead of `TextField.onChanged` is confirmed to work on iOS. Discussion resolved toward updating `TextField.onChanged` documentation to clarify IME composing limitations and recommend `addListener` for robust IME handling.
summaries.102568: Comments confirm the glitch is specific to ProMotion displays (iPhone 13 Pro physical device) and `CADisableMinimumFrameDurationOnPhone: true` does not fix it. Also confirmed on iPhone XS Max. One commenter noted the issue stopped occurring after upgrading to v3.10.0 with Impeller enabled on iOS. Assignee (@cyanglaz) was eventually unassigned due to inactivity; effectively stalled.
summaries.147772: Comments confirm reproduction on Android TV emulator on stable 3.19.6 and master 3.22.0-pre. Using `NavigationMode.directional` does not fix the problem. The original intent of `navigationMode` was to support TV/D-pad input. A community workaround plugin `native_textfield_tv` was published. Stalled with no framework fix.
summaries.125765: Comments confirm the issue reproduces only on iOS (Safari and Chrome on web), not Android. Testing on Samsung Galaxy S23 Ultra in Chrome did not reproduce it. Related to Samsung keyboard issues (#120351) but confirmed as a different bug. Stalled with no fix or PR.
summaries.91174: Comments confirm this is a long-standing alignment issue not fixed by prior PRs (#28189, #91804, #91998). The core problem is that `prefixIcon` cannot be reliably aligned with input text baseline across all configurations (with/without label, outlined/underline border, different platforms). Multiple community workarounds were proposed: wrapping prefixIcon in a `Column(mainAxisAlignment: center)`, using empty `BoxConstraints` for `prefixIconConstraints`, or using `prefix` with `InputDecorator`. No framework-level fix landed; stalled.
summaries.130011: Comments confirm the bug (magnifying glass stuck visible) reproduces on Android and iOS with `canRequestFocus: false` on stable 3.10.5 and master 3.12.0-pre. A related PR #130164 removed the `canRequestFocus` parameter in favor of using FocusNode directly, but did not fix the underlying buggy behavior. The original use case was disabling text selection without disabling suffix widgets. One commenter reported the issue could not be reproduced on 3.27.4. Stalled/possibly fixed.
summaries.130259: Comments confirm reproduction on stable 3.10.5 and master 3.12.0-pre on Android and iOS. The trigger is identified as the keyboard activation causing the floating `SliverPersistentHeader` to reset scroll position. @LongCatIsLooong was pinged for context. Stalled with no fix.
summaries.97506: Comments confirm the continuous cursor drag behavior is Samsung device-specific (Samsung A32, M22 with One UI 3.1), not reproduced on Xiaomi, Poco, Pixel, or older Samsung J5. A proposal was made to add a `cursorBehavior` API (e.g., `JumpingCursorBehavior`, `GoThroughCursorBehavior`, `CupertinoCursorBehavior`) as a developer option. No fix or PR. Stalled.
summaries.95010: Comments identify the root cause: the issue only occurs when Safari's URL bar is positioned at the bottom (default since iOS 15). On iOS 14 or with the URL bar at the top, it does not reproduce. A physical device user reported that in PWA (Add to Home Screen) mode, the bottom of the app becomes unresponsive after any text field focus/unfocus. Assignee (@mdebbar) was unassigned due to inactivity. One later commenter could not reproduce it. Status unclear.
summaries.72269: Comments confirm the cursor overlap with hint text reproduces on latest master and stable across all Flutter platforms (iOS, Android, web, macOS). The issue also affects `TextAlign.center`. Reproduced as recently as stable 3.7.7. No fix or PR. Stalled.
summaries.36910: Comments confirm the iOS VoiceOver accessibility focus issue (focus not returned to TextField after dismissing keyboard) reproduces on stable 3.3.9 and master 3.6.0-pre, not just the original 1.7 report. The same bug is noted in `SearchDelegate`, where dismissing the keyboard moves focus to the bottom or middle of search results. Stalled.
summaries.133662: Comments identify the root cause: the assertion requiring a `Material` ancestor fires unconditionally regardless of the `decoration` value, contradicting the documentation. @justinmc investigated and found that removing the assert allows `TextField` with and without `decoration` to work under `CupertinoApp` and `WidgetsApp` when proper localizations are provided. A fix was proposed (removing the Material ancestor assertion) and assigned to @Renzo-Olivares, but the assignee was eventually removed due to inactivity. Stalled.
summaries.78308: Comments confirm the vertical text misalignment when using `suffix` (not `suffixIcon`) reproduces on stable and master, and persists as late as 3.32. Community workarounds: use `suffixIcon` instead of `suffix`, or set `suffixIconConstraints: BoxConstraints(minWidth: 22, minHeight: 22)`. No framework fix landed.
summaries.115400: Comments confirm the selection handle flashing in the Android magnifier. The likely cause identified is a race condition in the BackdropFilter rendering, occurring intermittently. It was noted that `shouldDisplayHandlesInMagnifier` is already `false` on Android but handles still flash through. Additional related issues noted: toolbar positioned too close to the field (#separate issue) and handle clipped by keyboard (#120551). Issue was considered for shipping behind a feature flag but no fix landed. Stalled.
summaries.134330: Comments identify the issue as desktop-specific (macOS/Windows): on a password field, Chinese IME candidate list still appears and the input method is not forced to English mode, unlike native apps (Qt, Objective-C). Web behaves correctly (Chinese input disabled). Android and iOS also behave correctly. The bug was labeled desktop but no fix or PR was proposed. Stalled.
summaries.84341: Comments confirm that `autofocus: true` in a non-modal `showBottomSheet` does not steal focus from the background `TextField`, while `showModalBottomSheet` does work correctly. The distinction is that modal bottom sheets prevent background interaction. Reproduced on Android and iOS on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0-pre. Stalled with no fix.
summaries.137771: Comments confirm the console errors (null key event properties from autofill) reproduce on Chrome and Safari on stable and master; Firefox is not affected. The root cause is that Chrome and Safari dispatch empty keydown/keyup events during autofill with null `code`/`keyCode` fields, which Flutter's keyboard binding does not handle. No functionality issues result, only console errors. Still reproducible as of 3.29.3. Stalled.
summaries.74255: Comments describe the gap between Flutter's desktop context menus and native ones. Key developments: @justinmc landed #107193 making `DesktopTextSelectionToolbar` and `CupertinoDesktopTextSelectionToolbar` public and fully widget-based, allowing developer customization. PR #107193 merged. Many of the original issues (customization, button set, widget-based API) are now addressed. Remaining items (native blur/glass effect, native menu integration) are noted but no further active work tracked.
summaries.96092: Comments confirm the ATOK (Super ATOK ULTIAS on Fujitsu) issue is device-specific; the standard ATOK APK works fine on other devices. The original reporter identified that removing `FilteringTextInputFormatter` resolves the problem, suggesting the formatter interaction with this IME is the root cause. Another commenter notes the bug also occurs on any non-English keyboard when `enableSuggestions: false` is set, linking to #139143. Stalled.
summaries.99186: Comments contain a detailed platform-by-platform analysis of native vs. Flutter undo/redo coalescing behavior on macOS, Linux, and Windows. Key findings: on Linux, native undo is word-by-word while Flutter is more granular; on macOS, native coalescing is time-based; on Windows, the pattern is complex. Redo (Ctrl+Shift+Z) was noted as not working on Linux in Flutter. Stalled with no fix or PR.
summaries.19584: Comments identify the root cause: Flutter's ICU data (`icudtl.dat`, ~800KB) uses Chromium's Android ICU configuration which omits the CJK dictionary needed for word-break detection; including it would add ~1.9MB uncompressed. Proposed solutions include: using platform APIs (iOS `CFStringTokenizer`, Android `BreakIterator`), shipping different ICU builds per platform (as Chromium does), or allowing developers to select ICU features at build time. Reproduced on stable 3.19.5 and master 3.22.0-pre. Stalled waiting on an engine decision.
summaries.156183: Comments confirm the dead-key accent character deletion (pressing `´` then `a` produces `A` instead of `Á`) is specific to iPad Air with iOS 17.5 simulator using Portuguese (Portugal) keyboard; it does not reproduce on iOS 18.0. Testing on native iOS 17.5 (Reminders app) shows the same bug, indicating it is an iOS 17.5 simulator bug, not Flutter-specific. Set to low priority (P3) since iOS 18 fixes it.
summaries.104031: Comments confirm the issue also reproduces on Zebra TC52 and HP Chromebook x360 (Flutter 3.10.5), not only ARC++. One commenter notes that switching to another app and back temporarily restores key dispatch until the next TextField focus. The root cause is that the IME intercepts printable key events after a TextField is focused and does not release that interception, so `FlutterView.dispatchKeyEvent` is never called for those keys. No fix or PR. Stalled.
summaries.140508: Comments confirm this is a regression introduced by engine PR #40746 in Flutter 3.16, where closing the keyboard now also removes focus from the TextField (not the case in native Android). A Java-level workaround was shared: subclass `TextInputChannel` and override `onConnectionClosed` to be a no-op. Engine PR #49366 was merged to address the iOS side. A commenter noted difficulty re-showing the keyboard programmatically after this change; workaround using `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.show')` was provided.
summaries.158263: Comments confirm the context menu scales and rotates with the TextField transformation on Android and iOS (stable 3.24.4, master 3.27.0-pre). Root cause: `CompositedTransformFollower` applies the full transform matrix including scale and rotation when positioning the menu. A workaround is available: use `contextMenuBuilder` to apply the inverse transform to the toolbar. A framework fix is proposed by adding a `translationOnly` parameter to `CompositedTransformFollower`. A `workaround available` label was added.
summaries.117143: Comments confirm the keyboard unexpectedly pops up when starting a drag-selection gesture on Android (stable 3.3.10 and master). The scroll-jumping part was fixed on master but the keyboard-popup during drag remains. The correct behavior would be to defer keyboard display until drag ends. Reproduced on Samsung Tab A7 (Android 12) and Samsung A10 (Android 9). Stalled.
